phrases.intent_en: Ask where a specific store is
phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, pouvez-vous me dire où se trouve cette boutique ?
phrases.pronunciation: ex-kyoo-ZAY-mwah, poo-VAY-voo muh DEER oo suh TROOV set boo-TEEK
phrases.cultural_note: 'Boutique' in French refers to any small or specialty shop — it's the everyday word, not a fancy one. French shopping centers ('centres commerciaux') are structured similarly to American malls.

responses.french: Elle est au premier étage, près de l'entrée principale.
responses.english: It's on the first floor, near the main entrance.
responses.phonetic: el ay oh pruh-MYAY ay-TAHJ, pray duh lon-TRAY pran-see-PAL

responses.french: Continuez tout droit et c'est sur votre gauche.
responses.english: Go straight ahead and it's on your left.
responses.phonetic: kon-tee-NYOO-ay too DRWAH ay say soor vo-truh GOHSH

phrases.intent_en: Ask if something is on sale
phrases.text_local: Est-ce que cet article est en solde ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess kuh set ar-TEEK ay on SOLD
phrases.cultural_note: France has two official sale periods ('les soldes') each year — winter and summer — regulated by law. Outside those periods, asking about discounts ('réductions') is also common.

responses.french: Oui, il est réduit de trente pour cent.
responses.phonetic: wee, eel ay ray-DWEE duh TRONT poor SON

responses.french: Non, mais nous avons une promotion cette semaine.
responses.english: No, but we have a promotion this week.
responses.phonetic: non, may noo ZAH-von oon pro-mo-SYON set suh-MEN

phrases.text_local: Je voudrais retourner cet article. J'ai le reçu.
phrases.pronunciation: juh voo-DRAY ruh-toor-NAY set ar-TEEK. jhay luh ruh-SOO
phrases.cultural_note: Return policies ('politiques de retour') in France are more restrictive than in the US — having your receipt ('reçu') ready is essential. Mentioning it proactively smooths the process considerably.

responses.french: Bien sûr. Vous souhaitez un remboursement ou un échange ?
responses.english: Of course. Would you like a refund or an exchange?
responses.phonetic: byan SOOR. voo swet-TAY un rom-boors-MON oo un ay-SHONJ

phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous me faire un ticket cadeau, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-RYAY-voo muh FAIR un tee-KAY kah-DOH, seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: A gift receipt ('ticket cadeau') shows the item but not the price — the concept exists in France exactly as in the US. Asking with 'pourriez-vous' (could you) is the courteous register for retail requests.

responses.french: Bien sûr, je vous l'imprime tout de suite.
responses.english: Of course, I'll print it for you right away.
responses.phonetic: byan SOOR, juh voo lam-PREEM too duh SWEET

phrases.intent_en: Tell a store employee you're just browsing
phrases.cultural_note: In French shops, staff often greet you and offer help — 'Je regarde seulement' is the perfect polite way to decline without being rude. It's shorter and more natural than 'Je fais juste du lèche-vitrine'.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if they accept credit cards
phrases.cultural_note: In France, 'carte bancaire' (bank card) is the generic term for any debit or credit card. Chip-and-PIN ('puce et code') has been standard in France since the 1990s — far longer than in the US.

responses.french: Oui, nous acceptons Visa, Mastercard et American Express.
responses.english: Yes, we accept Visa, Mastercard, and American Express.
responses.phonetic: wee, noo zak-sep-TON VEE-zah, mas-ter-KARD ay ah-may-ree-KAN ex-PRESS
